reviews.review_description: I got this game for my brother thinking this would be a great game but when I got into the game the tutorial taught him how to use a sword but didn’t teach him anything about the bow. Once we got into the open world it was difficult to find what we were supposed to do. Keep in mind this game was 25 DOLLARS. I tried to help him play but he was so lost he started crying.

It’s a great game I like all the quests you get but
No one plays multiplayer anymore and it’s a shame since that was the biggest reason I bought this game🫤￼￼
I love playing this game. Its fun! After the tutorial it's easy to play. The crossbow is easy. I can play this one for hours. Don't pass this one up.
This game is stupid and it shouldn’t be so hard to pick up the sword. I’m trying to entertain a 5 year old and this game SUCKS. HE CAN’T PICK UP THE SWORD BECAUSE THE GAME WAS DESIGNED BY MORONS.

The horrible use of meta quest fov makes this game barely playable. In original fruit ninja you could see where all the fruits are coming from and react. Here it's impossible to play classic mode without spastically moving your head left and right every second because the game are is too wide for this fov. Horrible lack of proper game design.

I mean don't get me wrong I'm a psvr 1 veteran and I've played the original fruit ninja and that was way better than this one this one is just confusing per say.you move around in this one but on the fruit ninja 1 you just stood there and slashed fruits. Way more simpler than this over worked $hit
Tried so many times but can’t even pick up the sword or bow from the selection page

reviews.review_title: Get the first vr one or mobile.
reviews.review_description: If you want VR Fruit Ninja then get the first one. It cost less and is the better game out of the two.
His game is pretty sweet. I’m a little sad that we cannot adjust the angle of the sword a little better but it’s pretty dope.
Multiplayer is broken
The FN2 comes far more content interesting and addictive without changing its core

Look, I'm not sayin' this is trying to be Beat Saber AT ALL! -Nor does it have to be. -BUT... The precision in the motion controls is just not there, which does not make me feel like a ninja at all... You can argue that this an adaptation of a mobile game, but this is quite expensive for clunky controls; the swipes of my finger on my phone made me feel more in control than this does. There is still fun to be had here so, good job.
The tutorial is confusing and also a lack of game play.
I've played this game for hours. I didn't know it had so many levels and different areas to experience and play in. Don't let the bad review get to you, this is a fun game if you like fruit ninja!

In my first entry I got to a point where it wanted me to move my left controller forward but no matter what I did I couldn't get it right. I tried to quit the game and my headset went black. Could be fun if it worked maybe.
If you like fruit ninja youll love this there’s a great map great mechanics fun crossbow bow sword gameplay. I just don’t like fruit ninja that much
Not fun. Raging a sword feels too random

reviews.review_title: lovely new experience for friut killers
reviews.review_description: a walkable map, archery, crossbow, hunting annoying noisy golden fruits - that's a lovely game (at least, for the Horizon+ subscription; not sure if I would buy it outside the subscription) the navigation is confusing in the beginning, but I found my way around, now it's ok
This is the game that keeps me coming back for more! The music mini-game alone is well fun!
Galapagos is above ground Island. Great to work and walk around.

reviews.review_title: App cant be launched on quest 2
reviews.review_description: Two years ago I bought this app, now it cant be launched anymore and always black screen when trying to login. Seems quest 2 has issues with downloading updates and require users to doa complete reinstall. The game itself lacks some instructions and I found quite difficult to switch between armoring.

Controls don't feel great. It's so hard to grab things - even when I'm pointing my hand directly at it the game doesn't register this. Also seems to be one of those games where they've stuffed it absolutely full of everything they can think of - too much stuff.

Got this with Horizon plus subscription. It's quite fun and colorful with good graphics and nice sound effects. Not sure about long-term use though. I can see myself getting bored after a while. I'm not sure I'd pay specifically for it though.
I think it's a little underwelm8ng and can get boaring after a while. Ok game but wouldn't recommend
So annoying to start a game in  multi-player. After 20 min of turning around we gave up trying to start a game ..why so complicated ???
I love this game, before I got it I saw bad reviews but once I got it, I loved it so please get this game

reviews.review_title: Why is there so much hate?
reviews.review_description: Best game      money well spent

reviews.review_title: Basically steals every good VR idea
reviews.review_description: I'll update this once I play more

Got this bc I really enjoyed survival mode on the first fruit ninja so why not get the new updated version? I was surprised to find out this does NOT have survival mode. Not cool. Don’t buy this if you liked the first one.

Large empty world
Too much work to earn points to spend on nothing or for a weapon
The one npc has the most useless information 
i think he mentioned about a weapon cart but its no where, he lied.
some challenges are extremely short
a sword on the other side of a creek you cant access just to tease/troll the player￼
Terrible small timer & not enough fruit to get a bigger score

reviews.review_title: One of the best updated o.g quest games.
reviews.review_description: I’ve played fruit ninja before but I waited till it was on sale then I bought fruit ninja 2. If you want a workout and a good game you have both here. Also I would recommend thrill of the fight 2.

reviews.review_title: Just stick with the first one
reviews.review_description: Huge world for no reason. Bows aren’t as enjoyable or entertaining at the swords. The first one is the better option.
This is a good break from 2D fruit ninja and it’s so fun with the game modes and if you do you get w rizz

reviews.review_title: Without instruction it is useless for me
reviews.review_description:
Iam a young at heart senior and bought  out of curiosity an oculus VR .
Had the joy to experience it once. I bought Ninja Fruit 2 , because I have grandchildren ( 7 and 8 years old)  UnfortunatelyUntil now,  I was not able to use it properly .
I was looking on social media for a kind of manual  to understand how to deal with that game. 
Without result. 2 weeks have passed,so I can not return the sale.
Don’t forget that you have customers that have no experience .
Maybe you can give me an idea how to understand to play with the VR .

Too hard for small kids to navigate. Requires me to keep putting on the goggles to navigate, then put them back on kid. Ok for an adult that really loves fruit ninja, but the in-game navigation destroys the beauty of what should be the most fun and simple game on the planet.
I did the trial. Liked it. Bought it. Then found out the multiplayer is horrible after owning it for 5 minutes. Can't return because the trial counts as ownership time. Don't waste your money.

I only rate 4 because the multi-player aspect, hot garbage. Got with the homie to play together and it's just kids sitting around screaming. Made a private lobby and we weren't able to do much. It would be sick if we could explore the whole map together and find the apples, complete missions. But unfortunately it's a lobby or single player.
Also the blades have to be sideways or you smack the fruit. Not a comfortable swinging aspect. Other than that super fun and alot to do

My son has fairly severe autism and was used to the PSVR version where the swords just "stuck" to his hands.  He didn't have to figure out picking them up and keeping the buttons held to prevent dropping them. 

You could have kept it simpler for kids with special needs and small children or at least provided that option.

developer_response.body:
Hi Pete,

Thank you for the review. This is an appreciated insight for us and I apologise for the trouble your son's had due to the controls in FNVR2.

We've recorded this feedback, and while the game is feature complete, will be sure to consider this adjustment for future updates if possible, and for our future VR titles.

Thanks for your time and for sharing this review.

reviews.review_title: 😄Just as fascinating as the last one😄
reviews.review_description:
🍭Fruit Ninja 2🍭🔅▶️25o/o-off◀️🔅

Fruit Ninja 2 is an absolute blast! Slicing fruits has never been this exhilarating. The new power-ups are mind-blowing, and the multiplayer mode is pure chaos in the best way possible. The graphics are so vibrant and juicy that I almost wanted to reach into the screen and grab a slice myself. The variety of challenges keeps me on my toes, and the satisfaction of nailing a perfect combo is unmatched. I can't believe how addictively fun this game is. It's a holy grail of fruit-slicing madness!

I purchased the first game and found it BORING. I purchased this version and I'm greatly UNDERWHELMED! After following the lengthy setup instructions, I pursued the hard-to-locate arrows and managed to slice 3 peaches. I saw another one that was out of my reach and lost curiosity after trying to enter various challenges, to no avail. I cannot imagine WHERE these positive reviews derive because I'm perplexed about gameplay. Can someone please provide enlightenment and/or VIDEO demonstration on HOW to navigate this game? I abhor puzzles.

developer_response.body:
Hi there, sorry to hear you've run into difficulties finding all the content in Fruit Ninja 2. 

You can look for the big rings that are floating in the sky around the game to get your bearings, they are placed directly above the related game mode. When you head towards them any golden trimmed doors should open automatically once you get close. 

Once you have entered any of those areas for the first time you will also unlock the ability to teleport there from the Map panel on your Ninja Pad.

- The Fruit Ninja VR 2 Team

reviews.review_description: I can't get past the weapons room. What do ckilk to move to the game?

developer_response.body:
Hello Danya, you must finish the tutorial to get past the weapon room. To finish the tutorial you have to follow each step presented to you by following arrows or visible text in front of you. if you need further assistance please visit our discord will be happy to help🙂 .
https://discord.gg/fnvr2

reviews.review_description: This game is hard to play. I would not recommend this game.

developer_response.body: Hey there! We appreciate your feedback, and we're sorry to hear that you found the game challenging. We understand that clarity in goals and missions can make a big difference in the gaming experience. We're excited to share that in our latest update, we're added clearer goals and missions while rewarding you along the way. We hope these improvements will make your time in the world of Fruit Ninja VR 2 even more enjoyable. Thanks for giving us a try, and we hope you'll consider coming back to see the positive changes! 🥋🍉🔪

reviews.review_title: Vngs and other people are so annoying
reviews.review_description: Hdgeo
Super adventurous and very fun as a kid I love it and I recommend it for everyone a little pricy but it is worth it

developer_response.body:
Thanks a bunch for the glowing review! 🌟 We're thrilled to hear that you're having a super adventurous and fun time with Fruit Ninja VR 2. Your recommendation means a lot to us, and we hope you come back to enjoy the replayable content again and again, finding great value in the fun-filled experiences we offer!

By the way, we've got some exciting news to share.  The latest update brings a mission system rework that will introduce lots of different goals and challenges to keep the excitement fresh. So, even more adventures await you in the world of Fruit Ninja VR 2. Keep slicing and enjoying your ninja adventures! 🥋🍉🔪

reviews.review_description: It is very fun but I can’t figure out how to leave the dojo, as I just got my vr headset and I am in a small area.

developer_response.body: Thanks for the review! You can leave the dojo by turning with the right analog stick and holding the left stick to aim at the area you want to teleport to, then heading towards the door behind you. Hope you have fun slicing 😄

reviews.review_description:
✅ shorturl.at/joFG1 ✅

This game is awesome ❗️ Awesome environment to explore, many modes that’s are fun and intuitive, and cool unlockables 🤙 Totally worth checking out 🥷

🍓🍒🍌🍎🍍🍇🥑🍉🍊🍐🥭🗡️

I find this game pleasant to play but for me it seems to me that the content is very short and that it could still be improved, I count on the developers to add more content to it, especially considering the price paid to acquire it.
I have a few suggestions for this:
1- I would like the developers to think about adding special effects to the weapons purchased because there is no point in earning points to buy a weapon that is only aesthetic. (ARC that destroys several fruits once charged, ice saber that slows down time…)
2- add the special effects of the arcade mode to the level of the classic mode to make it more playable
3- create a mode for the counter-attack of the fruits because I really liked the concept but I find that only one bomb to lose its blades is excessive at the limit it is necessary to propose a series of 05 blades that it you have to catch in the middle of the game when you lose the one you used
4- increase the time of the archery challenges because a few seconds is very little to be able to appreciate a challenge
Hoping that my suggestions will be taken into account by the developers to make this game one of the most exciting. THANK YOU

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for taking the time to share your valuable feedback and suggestions! 🙌 We truly appreciate your support and enthusiasm for Fruit Ninja VR 2. Your ideas are fantastic, and we're committed to making the game even more exciting and content-rich, and your input is instrumental in achieving that goal. If you ever have more suggestions or want to elaborate further, please feel free to visit our Discord channel at https://discord.gg/fnvr2. We'd love to hear more from you and the entire Fruit Ninja VR 2 community. Thanks again for being a part of the fun! 🥋🍉🔪

Your appreciation for the hidden golden fruits and the atmosphere of the open world is delightful to hear. We're committed to providing an exciting and immersive experience, and your feedback is invaluable. Here's to many more hours of fruit-slashing fun! 🎯🌍🔪

reviews.review_title: Great fun for the whole family.
reviews.review_description: The game is a lot of fun. There are little fun things to discover. I just wish there was a little more meat on the bones. But a great way to be introduced into the virtual reality scene.

Very fun game I would highly recommend for kids and if you like getting free rewards like badges or achievements this is the game for you overall 5 stars oh and I forgot to mention there is voice chat but no one curses

reviews.review_description: After the update, you don't need to run praises on the big map! The game mode is also fun, but the juice effect and refreshing pleasure are not as good as a generation. Hope to optimize the juice popping effect and sound effect

Those 2 stars are negotiable; I'll increase once we resolve my issue. Singler player mode is working correctly, but multi-player mode keeps crashing. My screen is flickering and I can't move. I have to shut down my Meta Quest 3 just to exit. Brand new unit. Really like your game; please help!

developer_response.body: Hey there! The team has done and investigation and set an update live that should resolve this issue. If you are having troubles with the voice transmission after this update please check the app permissions and manually allow use of the microphone.

developer_response.body:
Thank you for sharing your enthusiasm for Fruit Ninja VR 2! 🍏📱 We're thrilled to hear that you've been a fan of Fruit Ninja on iPhone and iPad and that you find the VR version to be the next level of fun. Your support and excitement are greatly appreciated.

We're also delighted to hear that you appreciate the extra elements and our roadmap with planned updates to make the game even better throughout the year. Since your review, we've introduced the exciting crossbow update, along with new weapons, game modes, areas to explore, and a range of new missions. We're committed to keeping the fun of slicing and dicing alive, and we're grateful for your continued support in our Fruit Ninja community. Have a blast in the game, and we hope to meet you there! 🏹🌍🗡️🍊🍏🔪
